differentials.name: Global / regional hypokinesis with systolic dysfunction → ↓ LV ejection fraction + ↑ end- diastolic LV diameter
differentials.raw: Global / regional hypokinesis with systolic dysfunction → ↓ LV ejection fraction + ↑ end- diastolic LV diameter

differentials.name: conduction disturbances (in 23-30%): isolated bundle branch block to complete heart block
differentials.raw: conduction disturbances (in 23-30%): isolated bundle branch block to complete heart block

differentials.name: Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy
differentials.raw: Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (LV hypertrophy > 15 mm, scattered patchy midmyocardial involvement)

differentials.name: Arrhythmogenic right ventricular cardiomyopathy
differentials.raw: Arrhythmogenic right ventricular cardiomyopathy (RV dilatation + dysfunction, fibrofatty infiltration of RV, dyskinetic free wall of RV + RV aneurysm)

differentials.name: Myocarditis
differentials.raw: Myocarditis (patchy epicardial LGE in often inferolateral distribution, hyperemia)

differentials.name: Amyloidosis
differentials.raw: Amyloidosis (biventricular hypertrophy, global + diffuse LGE, LA enhancement, diffuse myocardial nulling abnormality)

context: Frequency:   5% clinically, 20-50% at autopsy Distribution:   basal portion of septum, LV wall N.B.:   rare in papillary muscle / RV Echo (nonspecific): √  wall motion abnormalities √  thinning of basal septum √  LV dilatation (30%) CT: √  cardiomegaly (25%) √  pericardial effusion √  ventricular aneurysm (10%) MR (75-100% sensitive, 77-79% specific): CAVE:   often contraindicated ← cardiac pacemakers (unless device is MR compatible) √  myocardial thickening √  nodular / patchy areas of high SI on T2WI / SSFP (bright-blood steady-state free precession) ← edema √  late gadolinium-induced enhanc
